lyrics:
Too young to say no, the summing begins
Toys are laid down, they track when we win
Obedient, cheerful, thrifty and brave
The rules are recited until we behave

Winners get rewarded, losers just ignored
Their deeds become legend, how many times they scored
Success, the bargain that the wanting disappears
When there’s nothing left to fear

We thrust the rest of life into these waning days
Knowing that the end grows ever close
No time to linger, “I’m not done”, the nag of keeping busy
Too few the hours spent on what we love the most

We fill the silence just to prove we’re here
Measure out the days in sweat and years
Hands stay moving, hearts stay out of sight
Living like the doing is the life

We thrust the rest of life into these waning days
Knowing that the end grows ever close
No time to linger, “I’m not done”, the nag of keeping busy
Too few the hours spent on what we love the most

If I stop now, will I disappear
Who am I when there’s nothing left to clear
Just a breath, just a moment, just this frame
Nothing earned - nothing needs a name

We thrust the rest of life into these waning days
Knowing that the end grows ever close
No time to linger, “I’m not done”, the nag of keeping busy
Too few the hours spent on what we love the most
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track features a sparse, intimate texture, built upon a clean, fingerpicked acoustic guitar and a breathy, forward female vocal, creating a melancholic and contemplative atmosphere. The energy is consistently low and gentle, with a flowing feel that is both soothing and slightly somber. The production is clean and spacious, allowing the simple arrangement to feel full without being dense, giving each element its own clear place in the mix. A distinctive quality is the subtle lift in vocal intensity during the chorus, which adds emotional weight without disrupting the track's overall quiet and reflective nature.

review.review_text: PCBender lands somewhere between a folk confession and a country-rock meditation here, the fingerpicked acoustic holding steady at a deliberate 86 BPM in D minor while the lyric does the heavier lifting. The chorus earns its place: 'No time to linger, I'm not done, the nag of keeping busy / Too few the hours spent on what we love the most' is a line that actually stings, and the vocal rises just enough to give it weight without breaking the track's quiet restraint. The arrangement stays sparse throughout, which is a choice that mostly works, though listeners who want a fuller payoff may find the final chorus lands softer than the writing deserves. Still, the closer 'Nothing earned, nothing needs a name' is the kind of honest late-verse turn that keeps this well above the middling.
